reasoning: Zimbabwe made an 'initial' payment of USD $3.1 million to a 'first batch' of 378 white farmers on April 9, 2025. This was out of a group of 740 farmers who had already been vetted and approved for compensation. This leaves 362 pre-approved farmers awaiting their share of the current disbursement cycle. The government has USD $20 million budgeted for these payments in 2025, of which less than 20% has been used. Historically, Zimbabwe's 'structured dialogue platform' meetings with international creditors (AfDB) occur in late April or May, providing a strong political incentive for the government to demonstrate continued progress with subsequent batches of payments before June 1. The use of the term 'first batch' by officials strongly implies that further payouts to the remaining 362 farmers are imminent and part of the same administrative process. While some farmer groups have expressed skepticism regarding the long-term bond component of the deal, the government is proceeding with those who have accepted, and the momentum from the first successful payout in decades makes a follow-up payment within the next 7 weeks likely.
